title: Reduction (opnorm-form) omits ker L ⊆ ker L_S — foundational equivalence unproven

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Where.** "Notation and basic facts": the reduction
\[
  \varepsilon L \succeq L_S \;\Longleftrightarrow\; \|\widetilde L_S\|_{\mathrm{op}}\le\varepsilon,
  \qquad \widetilde L_S := L^{\dagger/2}L_S L^{\dagger/2},
\]
is asserted "(by conjugation with $L^{\dagger/2}$ on $\mathrm{im}\,L$)". This is the
FOUNDATIONAL reduction of the entire proof (eq. (opnorm-form)), used in Step 1 / Claim
cl:rank-barrier to conclude $\varepsilon L \succeq L_S$ from a barrier/operator-norm bound.

**The gap.** Conjugation by $L^{\dagger/2}$ only sees $\mathrm{im}\,L$ and is blind to
$\ker L$. The direction actually used to conclude positive semidefiniteness,
\[
  \|\widetilde L_S\|_{\mathrm{op}}\le\varepsilon \;\Longrightarrow\; \varepsilon L - L_S \succeq 0,
\]
is FALSE in general unless $\ker L \subseteq \ker L_S$. Indeed, write an arbitrary
$x = L^{\dagger/2}y + z$ with $z\in\ker L$. Then
\[
  x^\top(\varepsilon L - L_S)x
   = y^\top\big(\varepsilon\Pi - \widetilde L_S\big)y
     \;-\; 2\,z^\top L_S L^{\dagger/2}y \;-\; z^\top L_S z,
\]
and the cross term and $z^\top L_S z$ only vanish (so that the operator-norm bound on
$\widetilde L_S$ controls everything) provided $L_S z = 0$ for all $z\in\ker L$, i.e.
$\ker L \subseteq \ker L_S$. If some $z\in\ker L$ had $z^\top L_S z > 0$, then
$\varepsilon L - L_S$ would have a negative direction even with
$\|\widetilde L_S\|_{\mathrm{op}}\le\varepsilon$, so the conclusion $\varepsilon L\succeq L_S$
would not follow.

**Status: the containment is true but never stated or proved.** $\ker L$ is spanned by the
indicator vectors $\mathbf 1_C$ of the connected components $C$ of $G$. Every $S$-internal
edge $\{a,b\}$ ($a,b\in S$) has both endpoints in the same component, on which $\mathbf 1_C$
is constant, so $(\mathbf 1_C)_a-(\mathbf 1_C)_b = 0$ and hence $L_S \mathbf 1_C = 0$. Thus
$\ker L \subseteq \ker L_S$ and the equivalence holds. But because the problem places NO
connectivity hypothesis on $G$, this two-to-three line argument must appear; otherwise the
reduction (opnorm-form), and therefore the final inference $\varepsilon L \succeq L_S$, is
unjustified.

**Fix.** Add a short lemma: "For every $S$, $\ker L\subseteq\ker L_S$ (component-indicator
argument above); consequently $\varepsilon L\succeq L_S \iff \varepsilon\Pi\succeq\widetilde L_S
\iff \|\widetilde L_S\|_{\mathrm{op}}\le\varepsilon$, where $\Pi=L^{\dagger/2}LL^{\dagger/2}$ is
the projector onto $\mathrm{im}\,L$ and $\widetilde L_S\preceq\Pi$ so $\widetilde L_S$ is
supported on $\mathrm{im}\,L$."

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved.** I replaced the one-line assertion of the foundational reduction
(eq. opnorm-form) in "Notation and basic facts" with an explicit lemma
(Lemma: *Kernel containment and operator-norm reduction*) and a full proof.

The lemma establishes, with no connectivity hypothesis on $G$:

1. **Kernel containment $\ker L\subseteq\ker L_S$.** Since $x^\top L x=\sum_{(s,t)\in E}w(s,t)(x_s-x_t)^2$, $\ker L=\mathrm{span}\{\mathbf 1_C\}$ over components $C$. Any $G_S$-edge $\{a,b\}$ has $a,b\in S$ in the same component, on which kernel vectors are constant, so $x_a-x_b=0$; hence $x^\top L_S x=0$, and as $L_S\succeq0$, $L_S x=0$. (Exactly the component-indicator argument requested.)

2. **Support of $\widetilde L_S$.** $\Pi L^{\dagger/2}=L^{\dagger/2}\Pi=L^{\dagger/2}$ gives $\Pi\widetilde L_S=\widetilde L_S\Pi=\widetilde L_S$, so $\widetilde L_S$ is supported on $\mathrm{im}\,L$.

3. **Equivalence.** Decomposing $\mathbb R^V=\mathrm{im}\,L\oplus\ker L$: by (1), $A:=\varepsilon L-L_S$ kills $\ker L$, so $A\succeq0$ iff it is $\succeq0$ on $\mathrm{im}\,L$. The change of variables $z=L^{1/2}y$ (a bijection of $\mathrm{im}\,L$, inverse $L^{\dagger/2}$) gives $y^\top A y=z^\top(\varepsilon\Pi-\widetilde L_S)z$, yielding
$\varepsilon L\succeq L_S\iff\varepsilon\Pi\succeq\widetilde L_S\iff\|\widetilde L_S\|_{\mathrm{op}}\le\varepsilon$.

This closes the gap precisely as the critic identified: the dangerous direction
$\|\widetilde L_S\|_{\mathrm{op}}\le\varepsilon\Rightarrow\varepsilon L-L_S\succeq0$ now rests on the proved containment, so the cross term and $z^\top L_S z$ over $\ker L$ genuinely vanish.

**Numerical verification** on a disconnected example (components $\{0,1,2\},\{3,4\}$, isolated $5$, $S=\{0,1,3,4\}$): confirmed $\ker L\subseteq\ker L_S$ ($\|L_S\,\ker L\|\approx3\times10^{-16}$), $\Pi\widetilde L_S=\widetilde L_S$, and the equivalence (PSD fails just below $\|\widetilde L_S\|_{\rm op}$, holds just above).

The label `eq:opnorm-form` is preserved, so all downstream references (Claim cl:rank-barrier, Step 1) remain valid.
